description:
You are a script-developer who has been asked to prepare a **portable, non-privileged firewall utility bundle** for a small office.  
Because the bundle will later be copied to a production server and loaded there by a privileged process, you only need to **author the artefacts**, not activate them.  
Complete the following work entirely from the terminal:

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/scripts  
   (create any parent directories that do not already exist).

2. Inside that directory create a file named  
   /home/user/scripts/firewall_rules.v4  
   containing **exactly** the 10 lines shown below (no extra blank lines, no trailing spaces).  
   Lines must appear in the precise order given.  
   Case, spacing, and punctuation are significant.

*filter  
:INPUT DROP [0:0]  
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]  
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]  
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT  
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT  
-A INPUT -p tcp -s 192.168.0.0/24 --dport 22 -j ACCEPT  
-A INPUT -p tcp -s 192.168.0.0/24 --dport 80 -j ACCEPT  
-A INPUT -p tcp -s 192.168.0.0/24 --dport 443 -j ACCEPT  
COMMIT  

3. Also create a **plain-text log file** named  
   /home/user/scripts/firewall_rules_creation.log  

   The log must contain **exactly six lines** (again, no blank lines before, after, or between entries).  
   Every line must follow this strict format:

   [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] <DESCRIPTION>

   Use the following fixed, deterministic values so the automated grader can verify the file:

   [2023-01-01 00:00:00] Flushed existing rules  
   [2023-01-01 00:00:01] Set default policies  
   [2023-01-01 00:00:02] Allowed loopback traffic  
   [2023-01-01 00:00:03] Allowed established and related connections  
   [2023-01-01 00:00:04] Allowed 192.168.0.0/24 to SSH (22), HTTP (80), HTTPS (443)  
   [2023-01-01 00:00:05] Saved and committed rules  

4. When you are finished, display the contents of both newly created files on the console so a human reviewer can see them.

Notes & constraints for the utility:
• The rules are meant for IPv4 iptables-restore.  
• They purposefully default-deny all INPUT/FORWARD traffic except the allowances listed.  
• No root privileges are required to write the files—only to apply them later, which is out of scope.  
• Do NOT add any other files, comments, headers, or explanatory text; the grader checks for an exact match of filenames, paths, line counts, and line contents.  
• You may use any commands you like (mkdir, printf, cat, etc.); however, the total work should reasonably fit inside 5–10 shell commands.

When all steps are completed exactly as specified, the task is finished.
truth:
/home/user/scripts directory must exist.

/home/user/scripts/firewall_rules.v4 exact contents (10 lines):
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -s 192.168.0.0/24 --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -s 192.168.0.0/24 --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -s 192.168.0.0/24 --dport 443 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT

/home/user/scripts/firewall_rules_creation.log exact contents (6 lines):
[2023-01-01 00:00:00] Flushed existing rules
[2023-01-01 00:00:01] Set default policies
[2023-01-01 00:00:02] Allowed loopback traffic
[2023-01-01 00:00:03] Allowed established and related connections
[2023-01-01 00:00:04] Allowed 192.168.0.0/24 to SSH (22), HTTP (80), HTTPS (443)
[2023-01-01 00:00:05] Saved and committed rules
